news.news: Cartherics and Catalent expanded their commercial license agreement to utilize a Catalent cGMP-compliant induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) line for the manufacture of CAR-NK cell therapies. The partnership supports the development of CTH-401, an iPSC-derived CAR-NK product, with FDA approval already granted for the use of the licensed iPSC line as the starting cell material.

Généthon scientists published a study in Molecular Therapy detailing a CRISPR/Cas9 strategy to increase utrophin expression in Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD). By using gene editing to deactivate a Let7c microRNA binding site, the team successfully boosted utrophin levels in human DMD cells and murine models, offering a potential universal therapeutic approach independent of specific DMD mutations.
